subject: English
period_schedule_display:
Period schedule:
  Row 1: 35 minutes × 3 periods = 105 minutes
Total: 3 periods · 1h 45min

periods.activity_title: Picture Walk: Duck, Hen and the Stream

phases.description: Teacher greets the class and introduces the chapter title 'Fun with Friends'. Students are invited to say one word that comes to mind when they hear the word 'friends'.

Picture walk: Teacher displays or points to each of the four sequenced illustrations on pp. 1–2 one by one, eliciting student observations before moving forward. Teacher reads the dialogue aloud ('Duck: Come dear hen…') giving each character a distinct voice, then invites students to echo key lines.

Picture Reading task (p. 1–2): Students observe the pictures silently for one minute, then share what they notice — who the characters are, where they are going, and what problem they face at the stream. Teacher guides with open prompts such as 'What do you see here?' and 'What happens next?'
Teacher summarises the picture story in two or three sentences and asks students to predict: 'How do you think the ducklings helped?' Students share predictions aloud before the next period.

periods.teacher_notes: This is the first class encounter with the text; take time with each picture before turning to the next so students build the narrative mentally. Watch for students who confuse ducklings and chicks — use the illustrations to distinguish them clearly. The oracy discussion task will follow in the next period.
materials: Pointer or ruler for picture walk

periods.activity_title: Friend Talk: Helping Each Other Across the Stream

tasks_in_class.task_brief: Discuss with friends — five oral prompts

phases.description: Brief recap: Teacher shows the last illustration again and asks one student to retell in one sentence how the chicks crossed the stream. This bridges from Period 1's picture walk.

Pair warm-up on prompts 1 and 2 (p. 3): 'How many ducklings and chicks can you see?' and 'Are the duck and hen friends — how do you know?' Pairs discuss for two minutes, then two or three pairs share with the class.

Whole-class guided conversation on prompt 3 (p. 3): 'How did the hen and the chicks cross the stream?' Teacher draws out the key idea that ducklings used their strength to help the chicks, connecting it to the picture-story theme of mutual help.

Personal-experience prompts 4 and 5 (p. 3): 'Have you ever helped your friend? Did you feel happy?' Students share in a circle or small groups; teacher affirms each response and models a complete sentence if needed.
Closure: Teacher invites one student to say the 'big idea' of the story in their own words. Teacher previews the word-work activity coming next period.

periods.teacher_notes: Encourage shy students by pairing them with a supportive partner for the first two prompts before opening to whole class. Prompts 4 and 5 invite personal sharing — accept home-language responses and help students restate in English without pressure. The word-work matching task will be covered in Period 3.

periods.activity_title: Animals and Their Young Ones — Match It!

tasks_in_class.task_brief: Match animals with their young ones

phases.description: Quick recall: Teacher asks 'What do we call the duck's baby? The hen's baby?' Students reply from memory, anchoring prior learning from the picture story.

Teacher writes the five animal names on the board and reads them aloud; students repeat. Teacher then writes the five young-one names in a scrambled column and asks students to guess matches before consulting the textbook.

Students complete the match task (p. 3) individually in their textbooks — Duck–Duckling, Hen–Chick, Cow–Calf, Cat–Kitten, Dog–Puppy. Teacher circulates, offering prompts rather than answers for uncertain students.

Whole-class verification: Teacher calls on volunteers to read each pair aloud; class signals thumbs-up or thumbs-down. Teacher closes by asking students to use two pairs in an oral sentence, e.g. 'The cow has a calf.'

periods.teacher_notes: Students often confuse 'calf' and 'kitten' — emphasise that 'calf' belongs to the cow and connect it to the Hindi/regional equivalent if helpful. This period completes all anchored tasks in Section A; Section B begins in Period 4. No homework is assigned as all tasks have been handled in class.

section_contributions.implied_lo: Student infers narrative sequence and character motivation using a wordless picture story as the vehicle.
section_contributions.section_context: Duck and hen travel to village fair; ducklings carry chicks across stream.

section_contributions.implied_lo: Student responds to guided oral prompts connecting picture-story events to personal experience using a picture narrative as the vehicle.
section_contributions.section_context: Duck-hen friendship; ducklings carrying chicks; student's own helping experience.

tasks_anchored.task_brief: Discuss with friends — five oral prompts

section_contributions.implied_lo: Student matches animal names to their young-one names using vocabulary from a picture narrative as the vehicle.
section_contributions.section_context: Duck–duckling, hen–chick, cow–calf, cat–kitten, dog–puppy matching.

tasks_anchored.task_brief: Match animals with their young ones

items.source_lo: Student infers narrative sequence and character motivation using a wordless picture story as the vehicle.
items.source_context: Duck and hen travel to village fair; ducklings carry chicks across stream.
items.item_stem: Look at the picture story on Textbook pages 1–2. The hen is worried when she reaches the stream. Why is she worried, and what does the duck do to help everyone cross safely?

teacher_guide.suggested_answer: The hen is worried because she cannot swim and does not know how her chicks will cross the stream. The duck says she has a plan, and the ducklings swim across while carrying the chicks on their backs, so all of them get to the other side safely.

items.source_lo: Student responds to guided oral prompts connecting picture-story events to personal experience using a picture narrative as the vehicle.
items.source_context: Duck-hen friendship; ducklings carrying chicks; student's own helping experience.
items.item_stem: Look at the picture story on Textbook pages 1–2. Tell your teacher: How did the ducklings show kindness to the hen's chicks at the stream? Have you ever helped a friend the way the ducklings helped the chicks? Tell what you did and how you felt.

expected_elements: Mentions ducklings carrying chicks across the stream.
Shares a personal helping experience clearly.
Expresses a feeling about helping the friend.

items.source_lo: Student matches animal names to their young-one names using vocabulary from a picture narrative as the vehicle.
items.source_context: Duck–duckling, hen–chick, cow–calf, cat–kitten, dog–puppy matching.
items.item_stem: In the picture story, the duck travels with her ducklings and the hen travels with her chicks. Match each animal from the story and beyond with its young one.
items.visual_stimulus:
Animal | Young one
Duck | Kitten
Hen | Calf
Cow | Puppy
Cat | Duckling
Dog | Chick

teacher_guide.suggested_answer: Duck – Duckling, Hen – Chick, Cow – Calf, Cat – Kitten, Dog – Puppy
